title: Zigzag the Dancing Robot and the Cornebyborn Dance
summary: Papa Mole brings a special surprise to Disco and Disca: a pocket-sized robot named Zigzag! The family spends the day teaching Zigzag how to perform made-up words, navigate pretend slides, and master complex dance moves like the 'Cornebyborn' and the 'Rainbow Disco.' Though Zigzag tries his best to mimic human movements, his batteries eventually run low, and he settles down for a nap after drawing a picture of his robot friend.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are all of those cards?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then let me show you what these cards are. Oh, there's one heart card, a star card, a face card, a star card, a face card, oh, a heart card, a star, a face card, a star card.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, now you be the narrator and I'll ever since I put them...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can put them back up on the shelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okie-doke. Are you ready for stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I need to get my position.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can I lay down on the floor?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that's a good place to do stars and stories. That's a good place to lay down because we can look up and see the stars. Here, I'll lay down right next to you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ding dong dong dong.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who is it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hello, Papa Mole, said Disco and Disca. What entertaining thing are you bringing to us today?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story then you'll find out, says Papa Mole.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco says, telling the story? Are we inside of a story right now?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, actually keep telling the story then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm just trying to tell you to keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You weren't saying what Papa Mole said or what Disco said? Yeah, that's true. Well, Papa Mole said, I have a little robot to play with.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Did Papa Mole say that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He did.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Where?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It's right here in my pocket. Here, I'll come inside and we can play with it on the floor.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you show me how it works?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I just turn it on and then we can talk to it about what we want it to do and then it can do things. It can't do everything that we say, but it can do some things.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like what?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He took out the robot and it looked kind of like a little person with big feet and short legs and a round body and a little head with big eyes and a mouth and two little arms. And he said, let me show you, said Papa Mole. Okay, robot, say a made-up word. And the robot said, bleep-bloop, flibbertigibbet. And Papa Mole said, okay, wave hello. And the robot waved its arm up and down and went, hello, hello.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And what did Disco and Disca say? So Disco said, Cornebyborn!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole said, what does that mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a dance move.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cornebyborn? I don't think the robot knows how to do that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can show you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Papa Mole said, well, that's a very nice dance.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's called Cornebyborn.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cornebyborn? And you have to, if you want the robot to dance, you have to tell it how to move its arms and legs.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cornebyborn. Come here robot. Actually, come here robot, actually come here robot. We need a name for him. Maybe we can name him Zigzag. Zigzag, actually.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the robot said, okay, my name is Zigzag. Here I come. And the robot walked over, bleep, bleep, bleep, bleep, to Disco and Disca.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can I show you?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you showing me how to dance?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, I'm showing you how to do the Cornebyborn. Can you do it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I will try. And the robot bent its knees, boop, and then straightened them out, boop, and went down and then up. And then it clapped its hands, bzz-clap, bzz-clap. And then it did the knee trick again, down, up, down, up, and then it did clap, clap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Did I do it correctly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, you did.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you for teaching me. What else would you like me to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, Disco said that and then Disco said, do the slide down the slide.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Where is the slide?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pretend slide.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I will pretend to slide. And the robot walked forward, bzz-bzz-bzz-bzz, and dropped its body down lower and lower so it looked like it was going down a slide and it said, wheee, thump! And then it stood up again and said, I did it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then Mama Mole said, stand up robot and walk around and do exercises.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the robot said, okay, I will do exercises and walk around. Walk, walk, walk. Wait, what is an exercise?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Exercise is a move like stretching. Stretching. Can I show you how to stretch?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, please teach me.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You just stretch. Oh, I see!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the robot raised both arms high to the ceiling. Stretch, stretch.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mama Mole said that and Mama Mole said, bend your legs and sit down. Take a break and drink some water.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the robot bent its legs and then clonk, it sat down and said, I cannot drink water, but I will pretend. Drink, drink, drink, ahhh. Drink, drink, drink, ahhh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, is my water bottle in bed?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know. We'll find out after stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I don't think it's the end of stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, how about we have the moles teach the robot how to do one more thing? And then it'll be the end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can you do a Rainbow Disco?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A Rainbow Disco? What is that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's a...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the robot said, wow, that is a complicated dance move. I think maybe only people can do that, but I could try. And it started to jump up and down and hop around on its legs and wave its hands in the air and spin and it went faster and faster. Dance, dance, dance, dance, dance, dance. Oh no! And then it toppled over, bonk! And then it said, oh dear, I have fallen down while trying to dance. Maybe I should leave dancing to the humans. And then it stood up again.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then Disco said, can you please draw with your fingernails on the sketchboard?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, I can draw with my fingers. I do not have fingernails because I am a robot. What would you like me to draw?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A robot friend.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Like a Nana robot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes, I do have a Nana robot. And so the robot drew a set of squares and rectangles that looked just like a robot. And he said, this is my Nana robot. But now my batteries have run out, so I must recharge and go to sleep. And the robot sat down and plugged himself in and said, zzzzz. The end of stars and stories?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Disca
characters.description: One of the characters visited by Papa Mole.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Mama Mole
characters.description: A mindful member of the mole family who guides Disco on contemplative nature walks along the sidewalk’s edge, illuminating their path with a steadying headlamp. Pairing firm yet nurturing instructions with vital maintenance exercises to ensure the robot’s long-term mechanical well-being, they eventually retreat from the outdoors to join the communal deep sleep in the new living room.

characters.name: Zigzag
characters.description: A small robot with big eyes and short legs who tries to learn human activities and dances.

characters.name: Nana Robot
characters.description: A robot friend that Zigzag draws on a sketchboard.

places.name: Mole House
places.description: Uncle Grisco’s subterranean sanctum, a sprawling burrow of packed earth and reinforced timber, has been reimagined as a vibrant communal hub. Beneath its low, amber-lit ceilings, the scent of damp clay mingles with the ozone of a whirring robot that serves as both a metallic playmate and a rhythmic guide. Here, amidst the clatter of clockwork and repurposed scrap, the group gathers to master intricate new dances, their shadows flickering against the cavern walls in a spirited fusion of technology and grace.
